---
title: "Group 4 QIIME2 Workflow"
author: "Simar Jawanda"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    number_sections: false
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  eval = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE
)
```

# Overview

This file documents the QIIME2 workflow used for Group 4. Group 4 contains the paired-end BioProject `PRJNA1060792`.

# Step 1: Set Project Directory and QIIME2 Container

```bash
PROJ='/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/PRJNA1060792'; cd "$PROJ"
docker pull quay.io/qiime2/amplicon:2026.1
qiime_docker () { docker run --rm -it --user "$(id -u):$(id -g)" -v "$PROJ:/data" -w /data quay.io/qiime2/amplicon:2026.1 qiime "$@"; }
```

# Step 2: Prepare FASTQ Files and Manifest

```bash
cd "$PROJ/fastq"; for f in SRR*_1.fastq SRR*_2.fastq; do [ -e "$f" ] || continue; [ -f "$f.gz" ] || gzip -c "$f" > "$f.gz"; done; cd "$PROJ"
mkdir -p "$PROJ/manifests"
{
  printf "sample-id\tforward-absolute-filepath\treverse-absolute-filepath\n"
  for f in "$PROJ"/fastq/SRR*_1.fastq.gz; do
    [ -e "$f" ] || continue
    s=$(basename "$f" _1.fastq.gz)
    r="$PROJ/fastq/${s}_2.fastq.gz"
    [ -f "$r" ] && printf "%s\t/data/fastq/%s_1.fastq.gz\t/data/fastq/%s_2.fastq.gz\n" "$s" "$s" "$s"
  done | sort -V
} > "$PROJ/manifests/pe.tsv"
head -n 5 "$PROJ/manifests/pe.tsv" | cat -vet
```

# Step 3: Import Reads and Summarize Demultiplexed Data

```bash
qiime_docker tools import \
  --type 'SampleData[PairedEndSequencesWithQuality]' \
  --input-path manifests/pe.tsv \
  --input-format PairedEndFastqManifestPhred33V2 \
  --output-path reads.qza

qiime_docker demux summarize \
  --i-data reads.qza \
  --o-visualization reads.qzv
```

# Step 4: Trim Primers with Cutadapt

```bash
qiime_docker cutadapt trim-paired \
  --i-demultiplexed-sequences reads.qza \
  --p-front-f ACTCCTACGGGAGGCAGCAG \
  --p-front-r GGACTACHVGGGTWTCTAAT \
  --verbose \
  --o-trimmed-sequences trim.qza

qiime_docker cutadapt trim-paired \
  --i-demultiplexed-sequences reads.qza \
  --p-front-f ACTCCTACGGGAGGCAGCAG \
  --p-front-r GGACTACHVGGGTWTCTAAT \
  --p-discard-untrimmed \
  --verbose \
  --o-trimmed-sequences trim.qza

qiime_docker demux summarize \
  --i-data trim.qza \
  --o-visualization trim.qzv
```

Trim notes:

- 338F/806R primer matches were essentially complete.
- About 0.1% of reads were discarded in the final trim.

# Step 5: Run DADA2 Denoising

```bash
qiime_docker dada2 denoise-paired \
  --i-demultiplexed-seqs "PRJNA1060792/trim.qza" \
  --p-trim-left-f 0 \
  --p-trim-left-r 0 \
  --p-trunc-len-f 270 \
  --p-trunc-len-r 182 \
  --p-max-ee-f 2 \
  --p-max-ee-r 2 \
  --p-n-threads 8 \
  --o-table "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/table.qza" \
  --o-representative-sequences "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/rep-seqs.qza" \
  --o-denoising-stats "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/denoising-stats.qza" \
  --o-base-transition-stats "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/base-transition-stats.qza"

qiime_docker metadata tabulate \
  --m-input-file "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/denoising-stats.qza" \
  --o-visualization "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/denoising-stats.qzv"
```

DADA2 note:

- `270F / 182R` was chosen because it balanced read merging with reverse-read quality.
- After primer trimming, the amplicon length was treated as 428 bp.
- Using 270 forward bases and 182 reverse bases gave 24 bp overlap, which kept the workflow above the 20 bp practical threshold.
- The usual 10 bp buffer could not be applied to the reverse reads without sacrificing too much quality and losing mergeability.

# Step 6: Convert Metadata for QIIME2

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
import csv

inp = "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/metadata.csv"
out = "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/metadata_qiime.tsv"

with open(inp, "r", encoding="utf-8-sig", newline="") as f:
    rows = list(csv.reader(f, delimiter=","))

if not rows or len(rows[0]) < 2:
    raise SystemExit("metadata.csv still not in proper CSV table format")

rows[0][0] = "#SampleID"
rows[0] = [("orig_ID" if h == "ID" else h) for h in rows[0]]

with open(out, "w", encoding="utf-8", newline="") as f:
    csv.writer(f, delimiter="\t", lineterminator="\n").writerows(rows)

print("Wrote", out, "| columns:", len(rows[0]))
PY
```

# Step 7: Filter to PS-NP and Control Samples

```bash
qiime_docker feature-table filter-samples \
  --i-table "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/table.qza" \
  --m-metadata-file "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/metadata_qiime.tsv" \
  --p-where "[group_simple] IN ('psnp','control')" \
  --o-filtered-table "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/table_psnp_control.qza"
```

Filter note: excluded runs were removed using `group_simple`, leaving only `psnp` and `control` samples.

```bash
qiime_docker feature-table summarize \
  --i-table "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/table_psnp_control.qza" \
  --m-metadata-file "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/metadata_qiime.tsv" \
  --o-feature-frequencies "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/feature-frequencies.qza" \
  --o-sample-frequencies "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/sample-frequencies.qza" \
  --o-summary "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/table_psnp_control.qzv"

qiime_docker feature-table tabulate-seqs \
  --i-data "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/rep-seqs.qza" \
  --o-visualization "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/rep-seqs.qzv"
```

# Step 8: Filter Low-Abundance Features and Assign Taxonomy

```bash
qiime_docker feature-table filter-features \
  --i-table "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/table_psnp_control.qza" \
  --p-min-frequency 31 \
  --p-min-samples 2 \
  --o-filtered-table "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/filtered-table.qza"

qiime_docker feature-table filter-seqs \
  --i-data "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/rep-seqs.qza" \
  --i-table "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/filtered-table.qza" \
  --o-filtered-data "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/filtered-rep-seqs.qza"

qiime_docker feature-classifier classify-consensus-vsearch \
  --i-query "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/filtered-rep-seqs.qza" \
  --i-reference-reads "Group 4_old/silva-138-99-seqs.qza" \
  --i-reference-taxonomy "Group 4_old/silva-138-99-tax.qza" \
  --p-perc-identity 0.97 \
  --p-query-cov 0.8 \
  --p-threads 8 \
  --o-classification "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/taxonomy.qza" \
  --o-search-results "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/taxonomy_hits.qza"

qiime_docker metadata tabulate \
  --m-input-file "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/taxonomy.qza" \
  --o-visualization "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/taxonomy.qzv"
```

Filtering note:

- The sheet recorded a calculated threshold of `31.1004`, which was implemented as `31`.
- Sample frequency was used to guide the cutoff so the threshold would not remove too much real data.

# Step 9: Remove Mitochondria and Chloroplast Features

```bash
qiime_docker taxa filter-table \
  --i-table "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/filtered-table.qza" \
  --i-taxonomy "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/taxonomy.qza" \
  --p-exclude mitochondria,chloroplast \
  --o-filtered-table "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/table_no_mc.qza"

qiime_docker taxa filter-seqs \
  --i-sequences "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/filtered-rep-seqs.qza" \
  --i-taxonomy "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/taxonomy.qza" \
  --p-exclude mitochondria,chloroplast \
  --o-filtered-sequences "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/rep-seqs_no_mc.qza"

qiime_docker feature-table tabulate-seqs \
  --i-data "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/rep-seqs_no_mc.qza" \
  --o-visualization "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/rep-seqs_no_mc.qzv"

qiime_docker feature-table summarize \
  --i-table "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/table_no_mc.qza" \
  --m-metadata-file "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/metadata_qiime.tsv" \
  --o-feature-frequencies "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/table-no-mc-feature-freq.qza" \
  --o-sample-frequencies "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/table-no-mc-sample-freq.qza" \
  --o-summary "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/table-no-mc.qzv"
```

# Step 10: Collapse to Genus Level and Export Final Outputs

```bash
qiime_docker taxa collapse \
  --i-table "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/table_no_mc.qza" \
  --i-taxonomy "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/taxonomy.qza" \
  --p-level 6 \
  --o-collapsed-table "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/table-genus.qza"

qiime_docker feature-table relative-frequency \
  --i-table "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/table-genus.qza" \
  --o-relative-frequency-table "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/table-genus-relfreq.qza"

qiime_docker taxa barplot \
  --i-table "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/table_no_mc.qza" \
  --i-taxonomy "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/taxonomy.qza" \
  --m-metadata-file "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/metadata_qiime.tsv" \
  --o-visualization "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/taxa-barplot-no-mc.qzv"

conda activate biom_env

qiime_docker tools export \
  --input-path "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/table-genus-relfreq.qza" \
  --output-path "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/exported-genus-relfreq"

qiime_docker tools export \
  --input-path "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/taxonomy.qza" \
  --output-path "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/exported-taxonomy"

qiime_docker tools export \
  --input-path "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/table_no_mc.qza" \
  --output-path "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/exported-asv-table"

qiime_docker tools export \
  --input-path "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/rep-seqs_no_mc.qza" \
  --output-path "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/exported-rep-seqs"

biom convert \
  -i "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/exported-genus-relfreq/feature-table.biom" \
  -o "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/exported-genus-relfreq/genus-relfreq.tsv" \
  --to-tsv

biom convert \
  -i "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/exported-asv-table/feature-table.biom" \
  -o "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/exported-asv-table/asv-table.tsv" \
  --to-tsv

awk 'BEGIN{FS=OFS="\t"} NR==1{print "#OTUID","taxonomy";next}{print $1,$2}' \
  "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/exported-taxonomy/taxonomy.tsv" \
  > "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/exported-taxonomy/taxonomy_for_biom.tsv"

biom add-metadata \
  -i "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/exported-asv-table/feature-table.biom" \
  -o "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/exported-asv-table/feature-table_w_tax.biom" \
  --observation-metadata-fp "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/exported-taxonomy/taxonomy_for_biom.tsv" \
  --sc-separated taxonomy

biom convert \
  -i "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/exported-asv-table/feature-table_w_tax.biom" \
  -o "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/exported-asv-table/feature-table_w_tax.tsv" \
  --to-tsv \
  --header-key taxonomy

qiime_docker tools export \
  --input-path "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/denoising-stats.qza" \
  --output-path "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/exported-denoising-stats"

qiime_docker tools export \
  --input-path "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/rep-seqs.qza" \
  --output-path "Group 4_PRJNA1060792/exported-reads"
```
